title: AWS Finally Lets You Find Your Idle NAT Gateways - Last Week in AWS Blog
description: After years of complaints, AWS Compute Optimizer can identify idle NAT Gateways. At $35/month each plus data processing fees, finding unused gateways just got dramatically easier.

title: AWS Deprecates Two Dozen Services (Most of Which You've Never Heard Of) - Last Week in AWS Blog
description: AWS has done its quarterly housecleaning / "Googling" of its services, and deprecated what appears at first glance to be a startlingly long list. However, going through them put my mind at ease, and I'm hoping this post can do the same for you.

title: AWS in 2025: The Stuff You Think You Know That's Now Wrong - Last Week in AWS Blog
description: One of the neat things about AWS is that it's almost twenty years old. One of the unfortunate things about AWS is... that it's almost twenty years old

title: Amazon Promotes Malphas to Senior Vice President of Bad Decisions, Unveils 17th Leadership Principle - Last Week in AWS Blog
description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) today announced the promotion of Malphas to Senior Vice President of Bad Decisions, effective immediately. In this expanded role, Malphas will oversee the company’s strategic initiatives in byzantine pricing models, confusing product nomenclature, and Generative AI.

title: Amazon Q: Now with Helpful AI-Powered Self-Destruct Capabilities - Last Week in AWS Blog
description: Today 404Media released a truly stunning report that almost beggars belief.

title: The AWS Survival Guide for 2025: A Field Manual for the Brave and the Bankrupt - Last Week in AWS Blog
description: Welcome, intrepid cloud explorer! You’ve decided to venture into the AWS jungle in 2025, where the services multiply faster than your monthly bill. Forget those quaint relics like S3, EC2, and RDS that everyone always gravitates towards—despite being the lion’s share of AWS revenue, they’re practically stone tablets now when it comes to interest and attention. Let’s talk about navigating the real AWS experience.

title: AWS Certificate Manager Has Announced Exportable TLS Certificates, and I'm Mostly Okay With It - Last Week in AWS Blog
description: I don't think it's going too far to say that free TLS certificate offerings like Let's Encrypt and AWS Certificate Manager have taken encrypted connections mainstream.
